# Design Alignment Requirements Checklist: Crosslog macOS UI Design Alignment

**Purpose**: Validate that the UI design-alignment requirements are complete, clear, consistent, measurable, and ready for implementation review.  
**Created**: 2026-06-28  
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements. It evaluates the quality of the requirements, not the implementation.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are all explicitly removed legacy controls listed in one authoritative requirement and mirrored in acceptance criteria? [Completeness, Spec section FR-011, Contract section Obsolete Controls]
- [ ] CHK002 Are empty workspace requirements complete for topbar height, activity rail, drop zone, `Open Source`, and drag/drop entry point? [Completeness, Spec section FR-009, Contract section Empty Workspace]
- [ ] CHK003 Are topbar requirements complete for compact command field, sync icon, add-pane icon, and their relative placement? [Completeness, Spec section FR-004, Contract section Topbar]
- [ ] CHK004 Are pane workspace requirements complete for drag resize, width persistence, right-edge alignment, overflow, and independent pane horizontal scrolling? [Completeness, Spec section FR-012-FR-016, Contract section Pane Workspace]
- [ ] CHK005 Are file and directory pane header requirements complete for identity, current file, live indicator, offset, find, close, navigation, and active state? [Completeness, Spec section FR-017-FR-019, Contract section Pane Headers]
- [ ] CHK006 Are pane search and time offset popover requirements complete for compact content, pane-local anchoring, state isolation, Escape behavior, and focus return? [Completeness, Spec section FR-020-FR-022, Contract section Popovers]
- [ ] CHK007 Are theme and platform variant requirements complete for all supported appearances and shells without creating platform-specific product behavior? [Completeness, Spec section FR-023-FR-024, Plan section Phase 2]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK008 Is "compact command field" quantified with target dimensions or layout constraints sufficient for objective review? [Clarity, Spec section FR-004, Design Doc section Topbar]
- [ ] CHK009 Is "immediately to the right" defined clearly enough to prevent implementations that move sync and add-pane controls to the far-right window edge? [Clarity, Spec section FR-004, Contract section Topbar]
- [ ] CHK010 Is the distinction between persisted desired pane widths and view-computed fill widths explicit enough to avoid session-state ambiguity? [Clarity, Plan section Technical Context, Data Model section PaneWorkspace]
- [ ] CHK011 Is "no unused blank space to the right of the rightmost pane" defined with measurable layout conditions for fit and overflow cases? [Clarity, Spec section FR-014-FR-015, SC-002-SC-003]
- [ ] CHK012 Is "future controls unavailable" defined with acceptable states such as hidden, disabled, or clearly unavailable? [Clarity, Spec section FR-007, Contract section Activity Rail And Left Panel]
- [ ] CHK013 Is the time offset target popover requirement clear that Apply is the only persistent action and Close is not part of the target UI? [Clarity, Contract section Popovers, Plan section Gap Audit]
- [ ] CHK014 Are platform chrome variants defined with enough visual and structural detail for macOS, Windows, Linux, and Web? [Clarity, Spec section FR-024, Contract section Platform Chrome Interpretation]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK015 Are obsolete-control removals consistent between the spec, UI alignment contract, plan gap audit, and tasks? [Consistency, Spec section FR-005/FR-011, Plan section Gap Audit, Tasks section US1]
- [ ] CHK016 Are Activity Rail future-control guardrails consistent across spec requirements, design contract, and task phases? [Consistency, Spec section FR-006-FR-008/FR-025, Contract section Activity Rail And Left Panel]
- [ ] CHK017 Are pane search and time offset state-isolation requirements consistent with existing MVP per-pane search and offset behavior? [Consistency, Spec section FR-020-FR-022/FR-026, Research section Popover Decision]
- [ ] CHK018 Are empty workspace requirements consistent with the non-goal of adding a landing page or marketing-style screen? [Consistency, Design Doc section Core Principles, Spec section FR-009-FR-010]
- [ ] CHK019 Are theme/platform requirements consistent with the constraint that product behavior remains shared across Web and Desktop? [Consistency, Spec section FR-024/CR-003, Plan section Technical Context]
- [ ] CHK020 Are test migration requirements consistent with the reuse rule that unchanged behavioral expected results must not be rewritten? [Consistency, Spec section FR-027-FR-028, Contract section Test Automation]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK021 Are success criteria expressed with objective thresholds for obsolete-control absence, pane alignment, overflow, popover anchoring, themes, and platform variants? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec section SC-001-SC-007]
- [ ] CHK022 Can rightmost-pane alignment be objectively measured in both fit and overflow scenarios from the written criteria? [Measurability, Spec section SC-002-SC-003]
- [ ] CHK023 Can header no-overlap requirements be objectively assessed for long file and directory names? [Measurability, Spec section SC-004, Spec section FR-019]
- [ ] CHK024 Can pane-local popover placement be objectively assessed for left, middle, and right panes? [Measurability, Spec section SC-005, Spec section FR-020-FR-022]
- [ ] CHK025 Are theme and platform success criteria scoped to actual application surfaces rather than mockup chrome only? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec section SC-006-SC-007, Spec section FR-023-FR-024]
- [ ] CHK026 Is the "identify how to open a source in no more than 5 seconds" criterion tied to a documented review method or usability evidence expectation? [Measurability, Spec section SC-010, Quickstart section Required UI Checks]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK027 Are primary flows covered for empty workspace start, opening sources, multiple panes, directory panes, pane search, time offset, theme switching, and platform variants? [Coverage, Spec section User Stories 1-4]
- [ ] CHK028 Are preservation flows covered for existing source opening, live updates, directory navigation, sync, search, offsets, encoding, session restore, and safety behavior? [Coverage, Spec section User Story 5]
- [ ] CHK029 Are alternate layout scenarios covered for one pane, multiple fitting panes, and overflowing panes? [Coverage, Spec section Edge Cases, Spec section FR-014-FR-016]
- [ ] CHK030 Are future-surface scenarios covered so Directory Search and future rail controls cannot imply new MVP capabilities? [Coverage, Spec section FR-025, Contract section Left Panel Interpretation]
- [ ] CHK031 Are test migration scenarios covered for component tests, Web UI, Desktop WDIO, macOS XCTest, and unchanged non-UI regression tests? [Coverage, Contract section Test Automation, Tasks section Phase 5]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK032 Are long labels covered for pane title, directory name, current file name, offset tag, and status summary without ambiguous truncation rules? [Edge Case, Spec section Edge Cases, Spec section FR-019]
- [ ] CHK033 Are invalid regular expression and invalid time offset requirements specified as pane-local outcomes without cross-pane state leakage? [Edge Case, Spec section Edge Cases, Spec section FR-020-FR-022]
- [ ] CHK034 Are file deletion, same-name replacement, live updates, and browser monitoring limitations retained as explicit requirements after toolbar removals? [Edge Case, Spec section Edge Cases, Spec section FR-026]
- [ ] CHK035 Are theme changes while a popover is open and platform variant overrides included in the requirements or intentionally excluded? [Edge Case, Spec section Edge Cases, Spec section FR-023-FR-024]
- [ ] CHK036 Are drag-over empty workspace states specified to avoid layout shift and ambiguous source-opening behavior? [Edge Case, Spec section FR-009, Plan section Phase 4]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK037 Are accessibility requirements specified for icon-only controls, disabled future controls, structural regions, keyboard dismissal, and focus return? [Accessibility, Contract section Accessibility, Spec section CR-006]
- [ ] CHK038 Are performance requirements scoped for rendering, scrolling, search, session behavior, and required perf gate usage when affected? [Performance, Spec section CR-005, Plan section Phase 10]
- [ ] CHK039 Are security and read-only safety requirements preserved for log content, command/search text, popover values, filenames, and directory names as inert data? [Security, Spec section CR-001-CR-002, Plan section Read-Only/Security Model]
- [ ] CHK040 Are cross-platform validation requirements complete for local macOS and GitHub Actions Windows/macOS/Linux automated, UI, and build gates? [Non-Functional, Contract section Test Automation, Quickstart section GitHub Actions Validation]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK041 Are dependencies on `specs/001-multi-log-analysis`, `specs/002-redesign-activity-rail`, `docs/crosslog-ui-design.md`, and the HTML mockups explicitly ordered by authority? [Dependency, Spec section Authoritative Inputs, Plan section Reused 002 Baseline]
- [ ] CHK042 Are assumptions about Directory Search being future-scoped and not MVP behavior documented consistently? [Assumption, Spec section Assumptions, Contract section Left Panel Interpretation]
- [ ] CHK043 Are assumptions about not adding a UI kit, parser rewrite, backend, or platform adapter rewrite captured as enforceable constraints? [Assumption, Plan section Technical Context, Contract section Scope Boundary]
- [ ] CHK044 Are existing tests to reuse unchanged, tests to update, and tests to add traceable to specific requirements and stories? [Traceability, Plan section Gap Audit, Tasks section Phases 3-7]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK045 Is any remaining ambiguity around theme selection persistence resolved without creating new product behavior outside the alignment scope? [Ambiguity, Data Model section ThemeVariant, Tasks section US4]
- [ ] CHK046 Is any potential conflict between removing product-visible lifecycle buttons and preserving lifecycle test coverage resolved through test bridge requirements? [Conflict, Plan section Gap Audit, Tasks section US5]
- [ ] CHK047 Is any potential conflict between "Open Source" product wording and existing platform-specific source picker limitations documented? [Ambiguity, Spec section FR-008-FR-009, Quickstart section Required UI Checks]
- [ ] CHK048 Is any potential conflict between platform chrome variants and native Desktop window behavior explicitly limited to shell presentation? [Conflict, Spec section FR-024, Research section Platform Chrome Decision]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`.
- Add comments or findings inline.
- This checklist validates the written requirements and planning artifacts only.

## Post-Analysis Remediation Clarity

- [ ] CHK049 Is theme switching explicitly classified as product UI, runtime/session presentation state, or mockup/test-only behavior across spec, plan, and tasks? [Ambiguity, Spec section User Story 4, Spec section FR-023, Tasks section US4]
- [ ] CHK050 If theme switching is product-visible, are selector placement, accessible naming, persistence exclusions, and validation requirements documented without adding out-of-scope preference storage? [Completeness, Spec section FR-023, Plan section Storage, Tasks section T103]
- [ ] CHK051 If theme switching is not product-visible, are "user switches/changes appearance" phrases rewritten or bounded so they cannot imply new product functionality? [Clarity, Spec section User Story 4, Spec section Edge Cases, Contract section Shell]
- [ ] CHK052 Is FR-028's "new tests only" list consistent with the added guardrail coverage for Files behavior, Directory Search, platform default chrome evidence, and timed empty-state review? [Consistency, Spec section FR-028, Plan section New Coverage, Tasks section T019/T070/T114/T119/T121]
- [ ] CHK053 Are guardrail validation requirements for Files MVP-only behavior and Directory Search absence/disabled/inert states documented as design-alignment deltas rather than new product capabilities? [Scope, Spec section FR-003/FR-008/FR-025, Plan section Activity Rail And Left Panel Guardrails]
- [ ] CHK054 Are default Windows and Linux shell chrome requirements distinguished from presentation override requirements so reviewers can tell which evidence belongs to OS-specific UI gates? [Clarity, Spec section FR-024/SC-007, Plan section Phase 2, Tasks section T099/T119/T121]
- [ ] CHK055 Are US4 phase-completion requirements consistent with the constitution's corresponding-OS UI test rule for Windows, macOS, and Linux behavior? [Consistency, Spec section CR-006, Plan section Phase 2, Tasks section T109/T119/T120/T121]
- [ ] CHK056 Is the timed empty-workspace source-opening review method defined with participant role, starting condition, evidence format, and pass/fail threshold? [Measurability, Spec section SC-010, Quickstart section Required UI Checks, Tasks section T114]
- [ ] CHK057 Are accessibility requirements for icon-only theme/platform controls or presentation selectors specified if such selectors remain user-facing? [Accessibility, Spec section User Story 4, Contract section Accessibility, Tasks section T096]
- [ ] CHK058 Are final validation tasks T115-T121 traceable to specific success criteria and constitution gates rather than only release-readiness bookkeeping? [Traceability, Spec section SC-007-SC-010, Plan section Validation Gates, Tasks section Polish]
- [ ] CHK059 Are requirements clear that platform shell variants must not change source capabilities, file watching, session behavior, or MVP workflow outcomes? [Scope, Spec section FR-024/FR-026, Plan section Platform Interfaces, Contract section Scope Boundary]
- [ ] CHK060 Are checklist, analyze, and task artifacts consistent about whether unresolved A1/I1/C1 findings must block implementation or can be resolved during early implementation planning? [Ambiguity, Analyze findings A1/I1/C1, Tasks section Dependencies & Execution Order]
